seller_name: Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd
details:
DANGEROUS DRYSUIT, RISK OF DROWNING. DO NOT BUY I bought a dangerous and defective drysuit supplied on behalf of Alibaba.com by Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd under Trade Assurance Order 184474975001023119 (September 2023). Transaction 184474975001023119, Cost USD 252.00. The drysuit was used only seven times in 2024–2025 for boarding my boat from the jetty and was never submerged in water. Three weeks ago, I used it in waist deep water for approximately five minutes while loading my boat onto its trailer. I was horrified as the suit immediately started to fill with water as it had failed at all seams, allowing water to enter both legs and rise to my waist. I poured approximately half a pint of water out of each leg.
This is latent defect as it existed at the time of manufacture, it only became apparent later and was not caused by misuse or wear. My usage pattern supports this.
The product is dangerous as a leaking drysuit can cause can be disastrous through, cold shock and loss of buoyancy leading to risk of drowning,
A drysuit that leaks at every seam after <10 uses is a clear breach of Trade Assurance standards.
This is a serious safety hazard and demonstrates that the suit is unfit for purpose and defectively manufactured. There is no visible wear, damage, or deterioration to the taped seams. This is clearly a manufacturing fault.
Alibaba & Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd have ignored my on-line messages and only responded with sarcasm instead of assistance. They have not replied to my last two emails. This behaviour is unacceptable and violates Alibaba’s seller obligations.
DO NOT BUY A DRYSUIT FROM THEM YOU COULD DROWN
notes: seller_url verified — store subdomain waterproof-sport.en.alibaba.com matches company name exactly in search results; product_url is the seller's Dry Suit category page (best-effort, not a confirmed individual listing for this exact order); quantity 1 inferred from singular "a drysuit" — not stated as a numeral but clear from context; price USD 252.00 explicitly stated in review.

seller_name: Cangzhou Wings Of Liberty Sport Products Co., Ltd.
details:
Since 9 September 2025 that I placed my order with the seller Cangzhou Wings Of Liberty Sport Products Co., Ltd., I have been given a different delivery date almost every week. We are now at 25 January 2026, and the order has still not been delivered. Despite paying the full amount of 1,940 USD upfront, I have never received the product. To future buyers: BE CAUTIOUS with sellers who promise a 10% compensation for delayed shipping or claim that Trade Assurance will protect you. These guarantees proved to be fake, both from seller side and from Alibaba side. My order was marked as “completed” even though I NEVER received the item I paid for!!!!
Once payment is made, communication becomes inconsistent. You must constantly follow up yourself, and the delivery dates provided are repeatedly inaccurate. After several months of delays, the order is automatically closed by the system WITHOUT delivery. Adding to the frustration, you will be even be asked to write a review for a product that NEVER arrived, despite having paid nearly 2,000 USD!!!!!

seller_name: Ningbo Marvelous Import and Export Co., Ltd.
details:
Be very careful when purchasing anything from Alibaba. After previously purchasing from Alibaba on 2 separate orders, and I did find it to be quite cost affective and of good quality, with reasonably fast postage. It went down hill. When I tried to make a 3rd purchase from Alibaba (* note all 3 purchases were from different sellers *), the *S* hit the fan. The company I tried to make 3rd purchase from was: Ningbo Marvelous import and export Co Ltd.
I order a bulk lot of 5 of the same item at a total cost of $112.24AU including delivery and taxes. Went through payment process Using Papal in 4 interest free payments, *very important never pay directly with visa or any direct credit card and definitely do not pay via any type of bank transfer*. I could see that the Paypal payment was taking to long to complete and verify that order has been processed.
This is when I almost fell of my seat, I received a notification that the seller has updated payment amount. That new payment amount was not $112.24AU as previously stated 
But for $770USD, $1076.62AU I could not believe what I saw, I of cause canceled order immediately. I did receive notification from Alibaba that order has been canceled.
Thankfully purchasing using Paypal will give you the knowledge that payment will not go through and if it does you can definitely get it refunded.
I don’t know if this was a scam directly by this particular seller ( also I tried sending seller a very well worded message, but apparently my messages to this seller cannot get through as I have been blocked by the seller ) or if it is a issue with Alibaba itself, either way I will definitely never buy from this particular seller again, and be very cautious if I decide to buy from Alibaba again.
I am not saying that you should avoid Alibaba, but just be very careful about your payment method and check all notifications that you receive from Alibaba.
notes: Product not stated in review — "hardware tools" is inferred from the seller's Alibaba store inventory (tools, hand tools, pneumatic tools, hardware accessories). Seller URL verified: store at nbmarvelous.en.alibaba.com matches the exact company name. Quantity (5) and price ($112.24 AUD) are both explicitly stated. The attempted fraudulent charge of $770 USD / $1,076.62 AUD is documented in the review but was canceled before payment completed.

details: May of 2024 I started working on a pen kit design via Alibaba with Nanchang Keda Stationery Gifts Industrial. It usually takes 3 to 4 months to get an order from start to finish. I sent samples in June and paid a deposit on Sept 2024 and received samples just before Christmas, Dec 2024. The kits were so terrible I couldn't actually assemble the pen kit. The art on the bands was terrible and looked like one big smudge with no detail. I obviously rejected them. Alibaba played the Assurance game and communicated with the supplier. We went back and forth and once again Alibaba Trade Assurance Program has no teeth. They refused to force the company to refund my deposit even after extensive documentation an photos on the 8 major problems. The Black Titanium finish(if that is what it really was) had lines around it as if were being threaded. It was rough and not polished and yet had a gummy coating that never seemed to come off. Just a coule pictures of the pathetic attempt. Nanchang agreed to remake the kits, I finally received the much improved, far from perfect kit, in May of 2025. They looked very nice and 78% went together without problem. 22% failure is still much too high for samples that have been groomed prior to shipping. The transmission will not go into the end cap hole. The brass on all kits was also not the size of my original samples which will cause problems for customers who have already purchased my brass in bulk. Nanchang claimed that the last two fixed were easy and want another deposit to continue. Over a year and still nothing to sell, Alibaba is useless, as is Nanchange Keda...be wary, be very wary. I now purchase my kits outside of Alibaba and have had great results. Alibaba seems to be a magnet for unscrupulous vendors looking to get something for nothing.
notes: Quantity not stated — defaulted to 1 (custom sample order; flag for human review). Price not stated — MISSING. Currency not stated — defaulted to AUD per site convention. Product URL is best-effort: pen kits listing from the same verified seller store. Seller store URL confirmed via Alibaba search (jxkeda.en.alibaba.com). Reviewer explicitly mentions photos submitted to Alibaba Trade Assurance, so has_images=true.

details: DO NOT BUY from Alibaba or AliExpress if you expect after sales service. I recently purchased a car part through Alibaba sister company AliExpress (PO 8197387475556774) from TY-Carbon Made Store and it turned out to be very badly made. Junk product! No problem I'm thinking as they have a "Free Returns" policy if you are not satisfied. They approved my return and credit provided I send it (at my expense which I was told would be refunded) to their Sydney PO Box. I paid FedEx $79 who later informed me the AliExpress Post Office address would to accept the item due to too large. Have since found out Australia Post do not accept items over 105cm. Mine is 150cm. I requested an alternative delivery address several times but they never responded. They have now refused to refund my purchase or additional courier charge several time with emails like "We are sorry to inform that your appeal request was closed. We have revisited this request and found that this return parcel hasn't been delivered." and "We are sorry but your appeal has not been accepted since the parcel you returned has not been delivered to AliExpress warehouse". Well apparently it's my fault their delivery address wouldn't accept the parcel! Clearly they have not intension of honouring my credit and have a system that strings you along until you give up. DO NOT buy from these companies. They will rip you off at any opportunity!!

seller_name: Fuzhou Century City Hong Trading Co.
details:
Very disappointing – BEWARE OF THE FUZHOU CENTURY CITY HONG TRADING CO. TRADING ON ALIBABA I placed an order for two Oneplus 12 mobile phones from the Fuzhou Century City company on Alibaba which were promptly delivered to my address in Europe. When I opened the boxes first thing I noticed was that they contained Chinese charging cables which couldn't be used in Europe, and although I wasn't pleased about this I thought no big deal.
One of the phones was for my wife and I gave it to her, she switched on the phone and went through the process of transferring all the data from her old phone onto the new one and after she had finished doing this the Oneplus 12 just switched off and it wouldn't switch on again no matter what we tried.
I immediately contacted the saleswoman I had been dealing with at this Fuzhou company to inform her of the problem as we had only just received the phones less than an hour ago, she was totally unhelpful right from the start telling us to charge up the phone but the phone was already charged and was showing over 80% battery capacity when it switched off and wouldn't come on again. I left the phone on the charger for a couple of hours but it still wouldn't switch on or show any other sign of life apart from getting a bit warm. All this while I was exchanging messages with the seller who has absolutely ZERO customer service and she was implying that I was lying about the phone being dead and even insinuating that I had somehow damaged the phone, really terrible customer service and after care and I was getting really angry by this time as after spending $615 on a mobile phone the very least thing I expect is for that phone TO ACTUALLY WORK.
She was demanding all kinds of unreasonable stuff from me like "proving" the phone doesn't work, how the hell do you prove a phone doesn't work without you sending the phone back to them ? The last suggestion this woman made to me was to take the phone to a mobile phone shop and let them check it out, are you nuts ???? I just spent $615 on a brand new phone and I have to take it to a mobile phone shop ???
Completely AND TOTALLY UNHELPFUL company and staff to deal with and I have raised a dispute with Alibaba for a refund and my messenger has now been disabled from contacting the seller which doesn't look very promising as many customers complain that Alibaba always takes the side of their fraudulent sellers.
People need to beware when ordering from Alibaba and are better off obtaining products from more reputable companies with a fairer despute resolution process. I am still waiting on Alibaba to resolve my claim.
notes: seller_url verified — first search result directly names Fuzhou Century City Hong Trading Co., Ltd. at fzsjch.en.alibaba.com. product_url is best-effort — a matching OnePlus 12 listing was found on Alibaba but could not be confirmed as belonging to this specific seller. All other fields (quantity, price, currency) are explicitly stated in the review.

seller_name: Qingdao Flyer Agricultural Equipment Co., Ltd.
details:
Alibaba Trade Assurance is misrepresented – I've held off commenting for 2 years, but the tractor I purchased has cost me many thousands of dollars and is only now starting to go, although who knows for how long. The assembly was done by people who had no idea even though the company Quindao Flyer Agricultural Company STILL has gold status. The disputes person did process a small payment but there was nowhere to go after that even though the tractor was not fit for sale and I was badly cheated by the seller.
Buy off this website at your own risk. I may purchase small change bits but will never again spend big money as being cheated is all one can expect. Better to pay more for quality equipment from countries which offer a genuine warranty and which do not allow sellers to scam their customers.
The number of 1 star posts says it all. Alibaba has the ability to be the top seller in the world but will eventually fail because it supports its crooked sellers. Good luck to anybody who puts up the big money.
notes: Seller name in review ("Quindao Flyer Agricultural Company") is a phonetic misspelling of the verified Alibaba store "Qingdao Flyer Agricultural Equipment Co., Ltd." at flyeragro.en.alibaba.com — seller_url_status set to "verified". product_url is best-effort (one of several tractor listings from this seller; not confirmed as the exact model purchased). Price is MISSING — reviewer mentions "many thousands of dollars" but this appears to describe cumulative costs incurred rather than a stated purchase price. Currency defaulted to AUD (no country or currency indicator in review). Date is approximate ("2 years ago" from time of writing, not an exact date). Quantity 1 is explicitly stated ("the tractor I purchased").

details: Alibaba Trade Assurance is a SCAM – Alibaba trade assurance sides with manufacturer every time, without fail. You simply will not win! This whole site is full of scam businesses being protected by the platform under this pretense of being protected via trade assurance, oh little do you know, as soon as the money is sent, boom, your toast! Dishonesty and without Integrity this platform will dismiss your concerns while siding with substandard, ignorant businesses like Xiamen Kaituo Clothing Co., Ltd. Horrid attention to detail, hat sizings incorrect, Branding logos changed without authorization to printed on leather, not stamped, brim sizes incorrect and this is after samples were sent to the manufacturer, This Site is Toxic!

details:
Forget About Your Warranty – I purchased a Cadisen watch Model C8200. Looks great but it gains about 1.5 - 2 minutes per week. I contacted Dante J from Guangzhou Soyouth Wristwatch Co. Ltd. But after numerous exchanges I came to the realisation that their complaint/warranty department was there just to frustrate the buyer to the point of just giving up in frustration. 
Comments like 'Please provide video' ........ HUH!?? also: 
'I don't even know what you said. I want to know what's wrong with you. Is there any problem?' 
The last 2 comments were: 
'We were in good condition before delivery. Why did you say it was bad when you received it?' and:
'You can take it to the watch store to check if there is any problem. Our Watch has no problem before delivery, my friend' (all cut & pasted)
There were many more ridiculous responses like these, all totally unhelpful.
So if you buy a Cadisen watch don't expect any backup or decent customer service.
Once they have your money they want to fight you and will drop you like a hot potato.
notes: Price not stated in review; total_price set to 0, price_status MISSING. Quantity of 1 stated in source metadata. seller_url verified via web search (soyouthwatch.en.alibaba.com is the confirmed Alibaba storefront). product_url is a best-effort match — the C8200 listing from this seller found on Alibaba.com but cannot confirm it is the exact listing used for this purchase. Currency defaulted to AUD per schema rules (no currency hint in review).

Don't deal with the UU A Girl Store it's a bad company and Alibaba are no better.

details: There are so many scammers, Alibaba is an unsafe platform and no support either – Guangdong Plant Kitchen Cabinetry was advertising the vanity for USD$120 to $160. I tried to make a complain to Alibaba, but it is impossible to make any compliant or get any support from Alibaba.

seller_name: Guangzhou Yilong Cosmetics Co., Ltd; Guangzhou Haishi Biological Technology Co., Ltd
details:
BUYERS'BEWARE: Unreliable and unsafe platform – The ALIBABA platform has put me at risk as a buyer for several occasions from its unreliable sellers/ suppliers based in China. There is no use of using this so-called gold suppliers, verified suppliers, etc. Free and paid training were talking about this stuff but it appears that it is not going to protect consumers. The platform is also unsecured. The message that I sent to supplier was sent to another supplier. 
Their dispute process is useless, and I need to contact my credit card provider to recover payments made to the suppliers/ sellers based in China operating at ALIBABA platform. 
Another negative experience, I was chatting to a sale representative which appears to have good English but after I made a payment, I feel like I was speaking to another person with poor English. The supplier/ seller appears to use different names on ALIBABA platform as well under Guangzhou Yilong Cosmetics Co., Ltd and Guangzhou Haishi Biological Technology Co., Ltd., based on my research and who knows what other names they use on this platform to scam people. Be careful everyone. 
If you want to use the ALIBABA platform, expect be in a wild wild west environment where there is no rules to really protect you. 
Make sure you protect yourself in case this happen to you and can lodge a claim here in Australia for product which is not fit for purpose or failed to received the product or a similar situation.
notes: Two sellers named in the review (Yilong and Haishi); seller_url points to Yilong's primary Alibaba store (best-effort — multiple storefronts exist for both companies and the review does not specify which). Product is generic ("cosmetics") — no specific product named in review. Quantity defaulted to 1 — none stated. Price is MISSING — no amount mentioned. Currency defaulted to AUD (site default). Review is a platform-level complaint rather than a specific transaction account; no invented facts added.

details:
Unhelpful when it comes to buyer/seller disputes – Purchased products from a company called Shenzhen Yingtean Technology Co., Ltd. This was my second order from then, First order they sent me faulty Vape pens and gave us a hard time when it came to replacing them. The second order we made arrived 20 days later and all the flavours were wrong and upon scanning the QR code I found then to be unauthenticated, meaning they were counterfeit! The supplier [Name Removed] then kept stalling and gave me all sorts of excuses when it came to giving me the order that I requested! I had no choice but to lodge a dispute with Ali Baba but because the items I was purchasing potentially contained nicotine they refused to help me. 
Never ever will I order from this company and I suggest everyone else go through another supplier! 
I am now out of pocket 5 grand with fake products that I never ordered!

details:
Trade Assurance is all "Fake News". They protect their sellers in their own Countries. Will never win – Our first transaction with Alibaba was the worst experience we had in the last 20 years in business. We purchased some stock to the value of USD 1200 to try out a factory. About 60% of the stock arrived to us damaged and 10% of the stock was wrong – in other words 70% of the stock we cannot sell and it is going in the rubbish bin. We then opened 2 claims for the damaged stock, one with the factory (Shenzhen U Decoration Materials Co.,Ltd) Ref. 90675082001020686 and one with the shipping company. After multiple emails back and forth, Alibaba said the factory and the shipping company were not responsible for the damage as “the damage was not big and it is difficult to say who caused the damaged”. Nice one!
Regarding the incorrect stock that we didn’t order but we paid for, the factory actually admitted liability for the mistake in writing but Alibaba said the stock was close enough to what we wanted so the best they could do was 50% refund. Unbelievable!
Throughout this whole experience, it was very obvious to us that Alibaba was on their Country seller’s side at all times. And Alibaba simply refused to answer our clear and simple questions about their refund rules and other basic questions that we had. They have a very vague script and they stick to it repeating the same story in every email. Alibaba’s service is sub-standard and their impartiality is non-existent.
If you think Trade Assurance protected, think again. And buying product from Shenzhen U Decoration Materials, it should NEVER be on your list. 
We personally will stay away from Alibaba in future.
notes: 1) Seller URL is best-effort: search returned "Shenzhen U Decoration Material Co., Ltd." (singular "Material", not "Materials") at udecor.en.alibaba.com; name is very close but not an exact match — confirm before publishing. A second store (udecorate.en.alibaba.com) carries the same registered name; udecor chosen as primary because its listed products (vinyl wallpaper, window film, wall panels) best match a decoration-materials supplier. 2) Product name is generic ("decoration materials") — the review only says "some stock" with no specific product named; inferred from seller category. 3) Quantity set to 1 (default) — the review mentions "some stock" as a bulk trial but gives no unit count. 4) Trade Assurance dispute outcome ("will never win") is characterised as an inability to prevail, consistent with the review wording; no invented outcome detail added.

seller_name: Changzhou Youth Tools Mechanical Co. Ltd
details: Be very careful when using ALibaba – Had three of the last ten commercial orders go pear shaped. Each time I did everything to make a compromise with Alibaba seller but failed so had to escalate to Alibaba themselves. Alibaba found for the companies in each case. First order was a silicone sample that was sent with lids loose and it leaked and got contaminated. The second was some sanding belts that were made too short and the latest was an order that went missing. This last seller agreed to refund me by a given date so I waited and then they reneged. It appears there are Alibaba only companies representing individuals posing as much more than they are. When you do finally choose an Alibaba company use google to see if the company exists outside of ALibaba. If they don't then they are an Alibaba only company completely backed by Alibaba. I will no longer use Alibaba only companies and certainly will not pay via Alibaba (which suits the non-Alibaba (genuine) companies). My advice if you do buy through them. Pay with a creditcard and get strictly 28 day terms. If it goes over the time cancel the payment and get your money back or make another payment. Just don't wait for too long before cancelling. Alibaba think they are smart but all it does is lower confidence in fostering malpractices. Changzhou Youth Tools Mechanical Co. Ltd is the tin pot company who reneged. Good luck.
notes: Seller URL not found — three web searches returned no matching Alibaba store; store may have been removed. Quantity set to 3, representing 3 failed orders rather than 3 units of a single product. Total price missing — no amount mentioned in the review. Currency defaulted to AUD (no currency hint provided). Product name "sanding belts" taken from the stated product field; review also mentions a "silicone sample" but sanding belts is the more concrete product. Date is approximate based on "5 years ago" review timestamp.

details: The Trade Assurance is a scam and the Dispute Resolution Process is biased – Have had a dispute going for months over counterfeit 18kt gold, counterfeit platinum, fake Moissanite, fraudulent copies of GRA certificates. It’s cost me nearly $10,000 in products, testing equipment, independent valuations, import tax. The jewellery delivered was nothing more than cheap costume jewellery. Despite the overwhelming evidence I provided, the days and nights I sat and photographed every test on every piece, comparing them to what he sells (he tried to deny they were his and I’d swapped them for other fakes I’d bought). The metal was made of god knows what, the Moissanite was nothing more than we’ll cut glass and none of it could be sold especially as he’d gone to the trouble of having the metal stamped AU750 or PL950. The counterfeit GRA certificates, the spelling mistakes and grammatical errors would’ve been funny had I not been in a dog fight to get my money back. I even had messages where Pu Liang the thief, the conman, the fraudster from Quanzhou Pushi Jewellery AKA Platts Jewellery has admitted it was fake and he was apologetic. Then he turned into a vicious savage pig when he realised that if I didn’t get my money back I was going to make his life miserable. Regardless of all my evidence and the best gemological and independent metal analysis - the Dispute Team told me to negotiate with him and they would be of no further assistance. Then the executives of Alibaba got in touch when I posted a huge complaint. Initially in their emails they seemed as if they were interested in helping, in fixing my problem. After a few brief emails stating they needed more time, I continued to contact them asking where they were at with getting my money back....they stopped emailing at all. Thanks for that [name removed] and [name removed] (you’re both as useless as [name removed] and just as dishonest). Anyway I finally got my money back from my bank. ANZ and VISA I can’t thank you enough for working through this mess and getting justice for me. The idiot who sent me this rubbish wont even accept his crap back so it’s sitting in Brisbane waiting to leave for China. Because he won’t sign the consignment I’m about to tell DHL to destroy the lot. There are a few good companies that advertise through Alibaba and there are a lot of bad ones....don’t rely on the Dispute Resolution Team to help you, they won’t, don’t rely on the Trade Assurance Agreement, it’s worthless. If you buy from them only use a creditable credit card that will go to town on thieves like [name removed] and the bad eggs at Alibaba who get bribed by the businesses to deny your refund. YES YOU READ CORRECT!!! The Dispute Resolution Team are bribed by the companies in matters where the consumer wants a refund. They don’t bother to look at the evidence, they don’t care that the owner of the company calls you offensive names in Mandarin, they won’t ask the supplier to show any evidence that what they sell is of good quality, made of gold, platinum, Moissanite or even that what you ordered and payed good money for resembles what is delivered. I was offered $300US and keep the goods - I payed over $7000US for crap worth nothing even $200 and an import duty tax bill of $865. The thief even wrote on the invoice that this crap was worth thousands. I sent emails to the Australian Federal Police, Cyber Crime Unit, Australian Customs, Chinese Embassy and even the White House as Trump had warned Alibaba to stop counterfeit goods or he would stop US trade through Alibaba. They placed an anti-counterfeit group in Alibaba to appease him, but clearly they’re too busy taking bribes as well. Be very cautious spending money with alibaba because they will not refund your money....EVER!!! Your bank has to do the hard yards to get your refund.
notes: Seller URL matched via web search returning "Company Overview - Quanzhou Pushi Jewelry Trading Co., Ltd." at psjewelry.en.alibaba.com — marked verified. No specific product listing URL found; product_url left blank. Quantity not stated; defaulted to 1. Total price is approximate ("nearly $10,000") per reviewer's own words and includes not just product cost but also testing equipment, independent valuations, and import tax — flag for human reviewer whether to record as product cost only or total loss. Currency AUD from prompt hint, not explicitly stated in review text.

seller_name: Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd
details:
DANGEROUS DRYSUIT, RISK OF DROWNING. DO NOT BUY I bought a dangerous and defective drysuit supplied on behalf of Alibaba.com by Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd under Trade Assurance Order 184474975001023119 (September 2023). Transaction 184474975001023119, Cost USD 252.00. The drysuit was used only seven times in 2024–2025 for boarding my boat from the jetty and was never submerged in water. Three weeks ago, I used it in waist deep water for approximately five minutes while loading my boat onto its trailer. I was horrified as the suit immediately started to fill with water as it had failed at all seams, allowing water to enter both legs and rise to my waist. I poured approximately half a pint of water out of each leg.
This is latent defect as it existed at the time of manufacture, it only became apparent later and was not caused by misuse or wear. My usage pattern supports this.
The product is dangerous as a leaking drysuit can cause can be disastrous through, cold shock and loss of buoyancy leading to risk of drowning,
A drysuit that leaks at every seam after <10 uses is a clear breach of Trade Assurance standards.
This is a serious safety hazard and demonstrates that the suit is unfit for purpose and defectively manufactured. There is no visible wear, damage, or deterioration to the taped seams. This is clearly a manufacturing fault.
Alibaba & Xiamen King Q Waterproof Sport Co., Ltd have ignored my on-line messages and only responded with sarcasm instead of assistance. They have not replied to my last two emails. This behaviour is unacceptable and violates Alibaba’s seller obligations.
DO NOT BUY A DRYSUIT FROM THEM YOU COULD DROWN
notes: Quantity not stated in review — defaulted to 1. Product URL is a category listing page (Dry Suit group), not a specific product listing. Seller Alibaba store confirmed via direct search result matching company name exactly.
